pi_items.source: task.json metadata field; setup_task.ps1 file missing from repository

Web search: ELISA standard concentrations. Realistic geometric dilution series: 300→100 (3-fold), then 100→3.125 (2-fold serial dilutions). Within typical ELISA concentration range (0-3000 pg/mL). Contains 7 standards which aligns with ELISA best practices.
Web search: ELISA quality control standards. NIH/PMC and industry sources confirm 15% is the standard inter-assay %CV threshold for ELISA quality control. Intra-assay CV is stricter at 10%.

Web search: ELISA standard curve regression quality. R² = 0.98 is within acceptable range for ELISA assays. Literature shows 0.98-0.99 are commonly achieved in validated ELISA protocols. Preferred minimum is 0.99, but 0.98 is acceptable for most applications.

Task description and verifier logic analysis. Verifier checks: (1) Corrected OD = Raw OD - Blank Average (line 164), (2) Concentration from regression equation (line 168-169), (3) %CV from replicate values (line 192), (4) Flag for high CV samples (line 195).

privileged_info_summary: The task is a synthetic ELISA immunoassay analysis exercise with 40 patient samples. The standard concentrations [300, 100, 50, 25, 12.5, 6.25, 3.125 pg/mL] follow a realistic geometric dilution pattern. The CV threshold of 15% is the standard inter-assay quality control threshold in ELISA. The minimum R² value of 0.98 is an acceptable and commonly achieved value for ELISA standard curve regression. The task requires calculating corrected OD values, performing log-log regression analysis, determining sample concentrations, calculating %CV for replicates, and flagging samples exceeding the 15% CV threshold.
